title: Distributionally Robust Safety Under Arbitrary Uncertainties: A Safety Filtering Approach
abstract: 本文研究了如何在分布模糊条件下确保非线性系统的概率安全性。我们的方法基于备份安全滤波框架，该框架通过在高性能标称策略与经认证的备份策略之间进行切换来保证安全性。为处理模糊分布中的任意不确定性（即分布无特定结构且真实分布未知），我们采用基于Wasserstein模糊集的分布鲁棒（DR）建模方法。不同于在线求解高维DR轨迹优化问题，我们利用备份安全滤波的结构特性，将安全认证简化为标称策略与备份策略切换时间的一维搜索问题。我们进而开发了一种具有有限样本保证的采样认证流程，通过将经验失效概率与Wasserstein扩增阈值进行比较。通过杜宾斯车辆、高速赛车及战斗机三类系统的仿真验证，该方法展现了广泛的适用性与计算效率。
preview_text: In this work, we study how to ensure probabilistic safety for nonlinear systems under distributional ambiguity. Our approach builds on a backup-based safety filtering framework that switches between a high-performance nominal policy and a certified backup policy to ensure safety. To handle arbitrary uncertainties from ambiguous distributions, i.e., where the distribution is not of specific structure and the true distribution is unknown, we adopt a distributionally robust (DR) formulation using Wasserstein ambiguity sets. Rather than solving a high-dimensional DR trajectory optimization problem online, we exploit the structure of backup-based safety filtering to reduce safety certification to a one-dimensional search over the switching time between nominal and backup policies. We then develop a sampling-based certification procedure with finite-sample guarantees, where empirical failure probabilities are compared against a Wasserstein-inflated threshold. We validate our method through simulations across three systems, from a Dubins vehicle to a high-speed racing car and a fighter jet, demonstrating the broad applicability and computational efficiency.
one_line_summary: 提出一种基于分布鲁棒优化的安全滤波方法，确保非线性系统在分布模糊下的概率安全性。
